summary: Added --version/-V support to install.sh. Both flags print the version string (1.0.0) and exit 0 with no side effects. All three analyst-specified edits applied: VERSION variable, short-circuit case in argument parser, and usage text entry.
changes: install.sh: Added VERSION="1.0.0" variable after set -euo pipefail
install.sh: Added --version|-V case as first entry in argument parser with printf exit 0
install.sh: Added --version, -V usage line before -h/--help in usage()
tests: find . diff before/after --version run: no files created, modified, or deleted
shellcheck install.sh .agent/scripts/*.sh: passes with no warnings (exit 0)
bash .agent/scripts/validate.sh: all tests pass (Specialized pipeline, Installer, Engine config -- exit 0)
remaining_concerns: Version string is hardcoded as 1.0.0; no git tags exist to derive it from. Maintainer may want to adjust this to match the project's versioning scheme.
